name: ament_cmake_libraries

name: ament_cmake_python

name: ament_cmake_target_dependencies

name: ament_cmake_test
description: The ability to add tests in the ament buildsystem in CMake.

name: ament_cmake_ros
description: The ROS specific CMake bits in the ament buildsystem.

name: ament_index_cpp
description: C++ API to access the ament resource index.

name: ament_index_python

name: ament_clang_format

The ability to check code against style conventions using
    clang-format and generate xUnit test result files.

name: ament_cmake_clang_format
description: The CMake API for ament_clang_format to lint C / C++ code using clang format.

name: ament_cmake_copyright
description: The CMake API for ament_copyright to check every source file contains copyright reference.

name: ament_cmake_cppcheck

The CMake API for ament_cppcheck to perform static code analysis on C/C++
    code using Cppcheck.

name: ament_cmake_cpplint
description: The CMake API for ament_cpplint to lint C / C++ code using cpplint.

name: ament_cmake_flake8

The CMake API for ament_flake8 to check code syntax and style conventions
    with flake8.

name: ament_cmake_lint_cmake
description: The CMake API for ament_lint_cmake to lint CMake code using cmakelint.

name: ament_cmake_pclint
description:
The CMake API for ament_pclint to perform static code analysis on C/C++
    code using PCLint.

name: ament_cmake_pep257
description:
The CMake API for ament_pep257 to check code against the style conventions in
    PEP 257.

name: ament_cmake_pep8
description:
The CMake API for ament_pep8 to check code against the style conventions in

name: ament_cmake_pyflakes
description: The CMake API for ament_pyflakes to check code using pyflakes.

name: ament_cmake_uncrustify
description:
The CMake API for ament_uncrustify to check code against styleconventions
    using uncrustify.

name: ament_copyright
description:
The ability to check source files for copyright and license
    information.

name: ament_cppcheck
description:
The ability to perform static code analysis on C/C++ code using Cppcheck
    and generate xUnit test result files.

name: ament_cpplint
description:
The ability to check code against the Google style conventions using
    cpplint and generate xUnit test result files.

name: ament_flake8
description: The ability to check code for style and syntax conventions with flake8.

name: ament_lint_auto
description: The auto-magic functions for ease to use of the ament linters in CMake.

name: ament_lint_cmake
description:
The ability to lint CMake code using cmakelint and generate xUnit test
    result files.

name: ament_lint_common
description: The list of commonly used linters in the ament buildsytem in CMake.

name: ament_pclint
description:
The ability to perform static code analysis on C/C++ code using pclint
    and generate xUnit test result files.

name: ament_pep257
description:
The ability to check code against the style conventions in PEP 8 and
    generate xUnit test result files.

name: ament_pep8
description:
The ability to check code against the style conventions in PEP 8 and
    generate xUnit test result files.

name: ament_pyflakes
description:
The ability to check code using pyflakes and generate xUnit test
    result files.

name: ament_uncrustify

The ability to check code against style conventions using uncrustify
    and generate xUnit test result files.

name: ament_package
description: The parser for the manifest files in the ament buildsystem.

name: demo_nodes_py

name: dummy_robot_bringup

name: dummy_sensors

name: image_tools

name: intra_process_demo

name: lifecycle
description: Package containing demos for lifecycle implementation

name: logging_demo
description: Examples for using and configuring loggers.

name: pendulum_control
description: Demonstrates ROS 2's realtime capabilities with a simulated inverted pendulum.

name: pendulum_msgs
description: Custom messages for real-time pendulum control.

name: topic_monitor
description: Package containing tools for monitoring ROS 2 topics.

name: launch
description: The ROS launch tool.

name: launch_ros
description: ROS specific extensions to the launch tool.

name: launch_testing
description: Helper scripts for tests that use the ROS launch tool.

name: ros2launch
description: The launch command for ROS 2 command line tools.

name: libyaml_vendor
description: Wrapper around libyaml, provides the last version (1.8.0 rc) that ships with a CMake module

name: orocos_kdl
description:
This package contains a recent version of the Kinematics and Dynamics
    Library (KDL), distributed by the Orocos Project.

name: osrf_pycommon
description: Commonly needed Python modules, used by Python software developed at OSRF.

name: osrf_testing_tools_cpp
description: Testing tools for C++, and is used in various OSRF projects.

name: test_osrf_testing_tools_cpp
description: Test package, which uses things exported by osrf_testing_tools_cpp.

name: pcl_conversions
description: Provides conversions from PCL data types and ROS message types
authors: William Woodall Paul Bovbel Bill Morris

name: poco_vendor
description: CMake shim over the poco library.

name: rcl
description:
The ROS client library common implementation.
    This package contains an API which builds on the ROS middleware API and is optionally built upon by the other ROS client libraries.

name: rcl_lifecycle
description: Package containing a C-based lifecycle implementation

name: rcl_yaml_param_parser
description: Package containing various utility types and functions for C

name: builtin_interfaces
description: A package containing builtin message and service definitions.

name: lifecycle_msgs
description: A package containing some lifecycle related message and service definitions.

name: rcl_interfaces
description:
The ROS client library common interfaces.
    This package contains the messages and services which ROS client libraries will use under the hood to
    communicate higher level concepts such as parameters.

name: rosgraph_msgs
description:
Messages relating to the ROS Computation Graph.
    These are generally considered to be low-level messages that end users do not interact with.

name: test_msgs
description: A package containing message definitions and fixtures used exclusively for testing purposes.

name: rclcpp
description: The ROS client library in C++.

name: rclcpp_lifecycle
description: Package containing a prototype for lifecycle implementation

name: rclpy
description: Package containing the Python client.

name: rcutils
description: Package containing various utility types and functions for C

name: rttest
description: Instrumentation library for real-time performance testing

name: tlsf_cpp
description: C++ stdlib-compatible wrapper around tlsf allocator and ROS2 examples

name: rmw
description: Contains the ROS middleware API.

name: rmw_implementation_cmake
description: CMake functions which can discover and enumerate available implementations.

name: rmw_connext_cpp
description: Implement the ROS middleware interface using RTI Connext static code generation in C++.

name: rmw_connext_shared_cpp
description: C++ types and functions shared by the ROS middleware interface to RTI Connext Static and RTI Connext Dynamic.

name: rmw_fastrtps_cpp
description: Implement the ROS middleware interface using eProsima FastRTPS  static code generation in C++.

name: rmw_implementation
description: The decision which ROS middleware implementation should be used for C++.

name: rmw_opensplice_cpp
description: Implement the ROS middleware interface using PrismTech OpenSplice static code generation in C++.

name: ros_workspace
description: Provides the prefix level environment files for ROS 2 packages.

name: rosidl_cmake
description: The CMake functionality to invoke code generation for ROS interface files.

name: rosidl_generator_c
description: Generate the ROS interfaces in C.

name: rosidl_generator_cpp
description: Generate the ROS interfaces in C++.

name: rosidl_parser
description: The parser for ROS interface files.

name: rosidl_typesupport_interface
description: The interface for rosidl typesupport packages.

name: rosidl_typesupport_introspection_c
description: Generate the message type support for dynamic message construction in C.

name: rosidl_typesupport_introspection_cpp
description: Generate the message type support for dynamic message construction in C++.

name: rosidl_generator_dds_idl
description: Generate the DDS interfaces for ROS interfaces.

name: rosidl_default_generators
description: A configuration package defining the default ROS interface generators.

name: rosidl_default_runtime
description: A configuration package defining the runtime for the ROS interfaces.

name: python_cmake_module
description: Provide CMake module with extra functionality for Python.

name: rosidl_generator_py
description: Generate the ROS interfaces in Python.

name: rosidl_typesupport_c
description: Generate the type support for C messages.

name: rosidl_typesupport_cpp
description: Generate the type support for C++ messages.

name: connext_cmake_module
description: Provide CMake module to find RTI Connext.

name: rosidl_typesupport_connext_c
description: Generate the C interfaces for RTI Connext.

name: rosidl_typesupport_connext_cpp
description: Generate the C++ interfaces for RTI Connext.

name: opensplice_cmake_module
description: Provide CMake module to find PrismTech OpenSplice.

name: rosidl_typesupport_opensplice_c
description: Generate the C interfaces for PrismTech OpenSplice.

name: rosidl_typesupport_opensplice_cpp
description: Generate the C++ interfaces for PrismTech OpenSplice.

name: rviz2
description: 3D visualization tool for ROS.
authors: Dave Hershberger David Gossow Josh Faust

name: rviz_assimp_vendor
description:
Wrapper around assimp, providing nothing but a dependency on assimp, on some systems.
    On others, it provides a fixed CMake module or even an ExternalProject build of assimp.

name: rviz_common
description: Common rviz API, used by rviz plugins and applications.
authors: Dave Hershberger David Gossow Josh Faust

name: rviz_default_plugins
description: Several default plugins for rviz to cover the basic functionality.
authors: Dave Hershberger David Gossow Josh Faust

name: rviz_ogre_vendor
description: Wrapper around ogre3d, it provides a fixed CMake module and an ExternalProject build of ogre.

name: rviz_rendering
description: Library which provides the 3D rendering functionality in rviz.
authors: Dave Hershberger David Gossow Josh Faust

name: rviz_rendering_tests
description: Example plugin for RViz - documents and tests RViz plugin development

name: rviz_visual_testing_framework
description: 3D testing framework for RViz.

name: rviz_yaml_cpp_vendor
description: Wrapper around yaml-cpp, it provides a fixed CMake module and an ExternalProject build of it.

name: sros2
description: Command line tools for managing SROS2 keys

name: urdf
description:
This package contains a C++ parser for the Unified Robot Description
    Format (URDF), which is an XML format for representing a robot model.
    The code API of the parser has been through our review process and will remain
    backwards compatible in future releases.

name: urdfdom
description: A library to access URDFs using the DOM model.
authors: Wim Meeussen John Hsu Ioan Sucan

name: desktop
description: A package which extends 'ros_base' and includes high level packages like vizualization tools and demos.

name: ros_base
description: A package which extends 'ros_core' and includes other basic functionalities like tf2 and urdf.

name: ros_core
description: A package to aggregate the packages required to use publish / subscribe, services, generate messages and other core ROS concepts.

name: ament_tools
description: The command line tools for the ament buildsystem.

name: depthimage_to_laserscan
description: depthimage_to_laserscan

name: amcl
authors: Brian P. Gerkey contradict <AT> gmail.com David V. Lu!! Michael Ferguson

name: map_server
description: map_server provides the
authors: Brian Gerkey Tony Pratkanis contradict <AT> gmail.com David V. Lu!! Michael Ferguson

name: robot_state_publisher
description: ROS2 version of the robot_state_publisher package

name: depthimage_to_pointcloud2
description: A simple node to convert a depth image and camera info into a PointCloud2.

name: turtlebot2_amcl
description: Demo of turtlebot2 localization with amcl.

name: turtlebot2_cartographer
description: Launch files for turtlebot2 cartographer demo

name: turtlebot2_demo
description: Metapackage aggregating the turtlebot2 demo packages

name: turtlebot2_drivers
description: Set of drivers to talk to the kobuki base.

name: turtlebot2_follower
description: A simple follower using centroid in depth images.

name: turtlebot2_teleop
description: Launch files for turtlebot2 teleop demo

name: uncrustify_vendor
description: Package a newer version of uncrustify (0.61+, b6593c1, April 14th 2015).

name: depthimage_to_laserscan
description: depthimage_to_laserscan

name: diagnostic_updater
description: diagnostic_updater contains tools for easily updating diagnostics. it is commonly used in device drivers to keep track of the status of output topics, device status, etc.
maintainers: Austin Hendrix Brice Rebsamen Karsten Knese
authors: Kevin Watts Brice Rebsamen Jeremy Leibs Blaise Gassend

name: self_test
description: self_test
maintainers: Brice Rebsamen Austin Hendrix Karsten Knese
authors: Kevin Watts Brice Rebsamen Jeremy Leibs and Blaise Gassend

name: ecl_command_line
description:
Embeds the TCLAP library inside the ecl. This is a very convenient
     command line parser in templatised c++.

name: ecl_concepts
description:
Introduces a compile time concept checking mechanism that can be used
     most commonly to check for required functionality when passing
     template arguments.

name: ecl_containers
description:
The containers included here are intended to extend the stl containers.
    In all cases, these implementations are designed to implement
    c++ conveniences and safety where speed is not sacrificed.

    Also includes techniques for memory debugging of common problems such
    as buffer overruns.

name: ecl_converters
description:
Some fast/convenient type converters, mostly for char strings or strings.
     These are not really fully fleshed out, alot of them could use the addition for
     the whole range of fundamental types (e.g. all integers, not just int, unsigned int).

     They will come as the need arises.

name: ecl_core
description:
A set of tools and interfaces extending the capabilities of c++ to
    provide a lightweight, consistent interface with a focus for control
    programming.

name: ecl_core_apps
description:
This includes a suite of programs demo'ing various aspects of the
     ecl_core. It also includes various benchmarking and utility programs for
     use primarily with embedded systems.

name: ecl_devices
description: Provides an extensible and standardised framework for input-output devices.

name: ecl_eigen
description: This provides an Eigen implementation for ecl's linear algebra.

name: ecl_exceptions
description:
Template based exceptions - these are simple and practical
     and avoid the proliferation of exception types. Although not
     syntatactically ideal, it is convenient and eminently practical.

name: ecl_filesystem
description: Cross platform filesystem utilities (until c++11 makes its way in).

name: ecl_formatters
description:
The formatters here simply format various input types to a specified
   text format. They can be used with most streaming types (including both
   ecl and stl streams).

name: ecl_geometry
description:
Any tools relating to mathematical geometry.
     Primarily featuring polynomials and interpolations.

name: ecl_ipc
description:
Interprocess mechanisms vary greatly across platforms - sysv, posix, win32, there
  are more than a few. This package provides an infrastructure to allow for developing
  cross platform c++ wrappers around the lower level c api's that handle these
  mechanisms. These make it not only easier to utilise such mechanisms, but allow it
  to be done consistently across platforms.

name: ecl_linear_algebra
description: Ecl frontend to a linear matrix package (currently eigen).

name: ecl_manipulators
description:
Deploys various manipulation algorithms, currently just
    feedforward filters (interpolations).

name: ecl_math
description:
This package provides simple support to cmath, filling in holes
    or redefining in a c++ formulation where desirable.

name: ecl_mobile_robot
description:
Contains transforms (e.g. differential drive inverse kinematics)
    for the various types of mobile robot platforms.

name: ecl_mpl
description:
Metaprogramming tools move alot of runtime calculations to be shifted to
    compile time. This has only very elementary structures at this stage.

name: ecl_sigslots
description:
Provides a signal/slot mechanism (in the same vein as qt sigslots,
     boost::signals etc for intra-process communication. These include
     some improvements - they do not need a preprocessor, are fully type safe,
     allow for simple connections via a posix style string identifier
     and are multithread-safe.

name: ecl_statistics
description: Common statistical structures and algorithms for control systems.

name: ecl_streams
description:
These are lightweight text streaming classes that connect to standardised
     ecl type devices.

name: ecl_threads
description:
This package provides the c++ extensions for a variety of threaded
     programming tools. These are usually different on different
     platforms, so the architecture for a cross-platform framework
     is also implemented.

name: ecl_time
description:
Timing utilities are very dependent on the system api provided for their use.
	This package provides a means for handling different timing models. Current support

	- posix rt : complete.
	- macosx : posix timers only, missing absolute timers.
	- win : none.

name: ecl_type_traits
description: Extends c++ type traits and implements a few more to boot.

name: ecl_utilities
description: Includes various supporting tools and utilities for c++ programming.

name: ecl_config
description:
These tools inspect and describe your system with macros, types
     and functions.

name: ecl_console
description: Color codes for ansii consoles.

name: ecl_converters_lite
description:
These are a very simple version of some of the functions in ecl_converters
     suitable for firmware development. That is, there is no use of new,
     templates or exceptions.

name: ecl_errors
description:
This library provides lean and mean error mechanisms.
    It includes c style error functions as well as a few
    useful macros. For higher level mechanisms,
    refer to ecl_exceptions.

name: ecl_io
description:
Most implementations (windows, posix, ...) have slightly different api for
     low level input-output functions. These are gathered here and re-represented
     with a cross platform set of functions.

name: ecl_lite
description: Libraries and utilities for embedded and low-level linux development.

name: ecl_sigslots_lite
description:
This avoids use of dynamic storage (malloc/new) and thread safety (mutexes) to
     provide a very simple sigslots implementation that can be used for *very*
     embedded development.

name: ecl_time_lite
description:
Provides a portable set of time functions that are especially useful for
     porting other code or being wrapped by higher level c++ classes.

name: ecl_build
description:
Collection of cmake/make build tools primarily for ecl development itself, but also
     contains a few cmake modules useful outside of the ecl.

name: ecl_license
description:
Maintains the ecl licenses and also provides an install
     target for deploying licenses with the ecl libraries.

name: ecl_tools
description: Tools and utilities for ecl development.

name: eigen_stl_containers
description:
This package provides a set of typedef's that allow
  using Eigen datatypes in STL containers

name: camera_calibration_parsers
description: camera_calibration_parsers contains routines for reading and writing camera calibration parameters.

name: camera_info_manager
description:
This package provides a C++ interface for camera calibration
     information.  It provides CameraInfo, and handles SetCameraInfo
     service requests, saving and restoring the camera calibration
     data.

name: image_common
description: Common code for working with images in ROS.

name: image_transport
description:
image_transport should always be used to subscribe to and publish images. It provides transparent
     support for transporting images in low-bandwidth compressed formats. Examples (provided by separate
     plugin packages) include JPEG/PNG compression and Theora streaming video.

name: polled_camera
description:
polled_camera contains a service and C++ helper classes for implementing a polled
     camera driver node and requesting images from it. The package is currently for
     internal use as the API is still under development.

name: camera_calibration
description:
camera_calibration allows easy calibration of monocular or stereo
     cameras using a checkerboard calibration target.
maintainers: Vincent Rabaud Steven Macenski Autonomoustuff team

name: depth_image_proc
description:
Contains components for processing depth images such as those
     produced by OpenNI camera. Functions include creating disparity
     images and point clouds, as well as registering (reprojecting)
     a depth image into another camera frame.
maintainers: Vincent Rabaud Steven Macenski Autonomoustuff team Chris Ye

name: image_pipeline
description: image_pipeline fills the gap between getting raw images from a camera driver and higher-level vision processing.
maintainers: Vincent Rabaud Steven Macenski Autonomoustuff team

name: image_proc
description: Single image rectification and color processing.
maintainers: Vincent Rabaud Steven Macenski Autonomoustuff team
authors: Patrick Mihelich Kurt Konolige Jeremy Leibs

name: image_publisher
maintainers: Vincent Rabaud Steven Macenski Autonomoustuff team Chris Ye

name: image_rotate
maintainers: Vincent Rabaud Steven Macenski Autonomoustuff team

name: image_view
description:
A simple viewer for ROS image topics. Includes a specialized viewer
  for stereo + disparity images.
maintainers: Vincent Rabaud Steven Macenski Autonomoustuff team

name: stereo_image_proc
description: Stereo and single image rectification and disparity processing.
maintainers: Vincent Rabaud Steven Macenski Autonomoustuff team
authors: Patrick Mihelich Kurt Konolige Jeremy Leibs

name: compressed_depth_image_transport
description:
Compressed_depth_image_transport provides a plugin to image_transport for transparently sending
    depth images (raw, floating-point) using PNG compression.

name: compressed_image_transport
description:
Compressed_image_transport provides a plugin to image_transport for transparently sending images
    encoded as JPEG or PNG.

name: image_transport_plugins
description:
A set of plugins for publishing and subscribing to sensor_msgs/Image topics
    in representations other than raw pixel data. For example, for viewing a
    stream of images off-robot, a video codec will give much lower bandwidth
    and latency. For low frame rate tranport of high-definition images, you
    might prefer sending them as JPEG or PNG-compressed form.

name: theora_image_transport
description:
Theora_image_transport provides a plugin to image_transport for
    transparently sending an image stream encoded with the Theora codec.

name: laser_assembler
description: Provides nodes to assemble point clouds from either LaserScan or PointCloud messages

name: laser_geometry
description:
This package contains a class for converting from a 2D laser scan as defined by
    sensor_msgs/LaserScan into a point cloud as defined by sensor_msgs/PointCloud
    or sensor_msgs/PointCloud2. In particular, it contains functionality to account
    for the skew resulting from moving robots or tilting laser scanners.
authors: Dave Hershberger Tully Foote Radu Bogdan Rusu

name: nmea_msgs
description: The nmea_msgs package contains messages related to data in the NMEA format.

name: nmea_navsat_driver
description:
Package to parse NMEA strings and publish a very simple GPS message. Does not
        require or use the GPSD deamon.

name: pcl_conversions
description: Provides conversions from PCL data types and ROS message types

name: pcl_ros
description:
PCL (Point Cloud Library) ROS interface stack. PCL-ROS is the preferred
  bridge for 3D applications involving n-D Point Clouds and 3D geometry
  processing in ROS.
authors: Open Perception Julius Kammerl William Woodall

name: perception_pcl
description:
PCL (Point Cloud Library) ROS interface stack. PCL-ROS is the preferred
  bridge for 3D applications involving n-D Point Clouds and 3D geometry
  processing in ROS.
authors: Open Perception William Woodall Julius Kammerl

name: pluginlib
description:
The pluginlib package provides tools for writing and dynamically loading plugins using the ROS build infrastructure.
    To work, these tools require plugin providers to register their plugins in the package.xml of their package.
authors: Eitan Marder-Eppstein Tully Foote Dirk Thomas Mirza Shah

name: python_qt_binding
description:
This stack provides Python bindings for Qt.
    There are two providers: pyside and pyqt.  PySide2 is available under
    the GPL, LGPL and a commercial license.  PyQt is released under the GPL.

    Both the bindings and tools to build bindings are included from each
    available provider.  For PySide, it is called "Shiboken".  For PyQt,
    this is called "SIP".

    Also provided is adapter code to make the user's Python code
    independent of which binding provider was actually used which makes
    it very easy to switch between these.
authors: Dave Hershberger Dorian Scholz Dirk Thomas Stephen Brawner

name: qt_dotgraph
description: qt_dotgraph provides helpers to work with dot graphs.

name: qt_gui
description:
qt_gui provides the infrastructure for an integrated graphical user interface based on Qt.
    It is extensible with Python- and C++-based plugins (implemented in separate packages) which can contribute arbitrary widgets.
    It requires either PyQt or PySide bindings.

name: qt_gui_app
description: qt_gui_app provides the main to start an instance of the integrated graphical user interface provided by qt_gui.

name: qt_gui_core
description: Integration of the ROS package system and ROS-specific plugins for a Qt-based GUI.

name: qt_gui_cpp
description:
qt_gui_cpp provides the foundation for C++-bindings for qt_gui and creates bindings for every generator available.
    At least one specific binding must be available in order to use C++-plugins.

name: qt_gui_py_common
description: qt_gui_py_common provides common functionality for GUI plugins written in Python.

name: robot_localization
description: Provides nonlinear state estimation through sensor fusion of an abritrary number of sensors.

name: robot_state_publisher
description: ROS2 version of the robot_state_publisher package

name: rqt
description: rqt is a Qt-based framework for GUI development for ROS. It consists of three parts/metapackages
authors: Dirk Thomas Dorian Scholz Aaron Blasdel

name: rqt_gui
description: rqt_gui provides the main to start an instance of the ROS integrated graphical user interface provided by qt_gui.

name: rqt_gui_cpp
description: rqt_gui_cpp enables GUI plugins to use the C++ client library for ROS.

name: rqt_gui_py
description: rqt_gui_py enables GUI plugins to use the Python client library for ROS.

name: rqt_py_common
description:
rqt_py_common provides common functionality for rqt plugins written in Python.
    Despite no plugin is provided, this package is part of the rqt_common_plugins
    repository to keep refactoring generic functionality from these common plugins
    into this package as easy as possible.

    Functionality included in this package should cover generic ROS concepts and
    should not introduce any special dependencies beside "ros_base".

name: velodyne
description: Basic ROS support for the Velodyne 3D LIDARs.

name: velodyne_driver
description: ROS device driver for Velodyne 3D LIDARs.
authors: Jack O'Quin Patrick Beeson Michael Quinlan Yaxin Liu

name: velodyne_laserscan
description: Extract a single ring of a Velodyne PointCloud2 and publish it as a LaserScan message

name: velodyne_msgs
description: ROS message definitions for Velodyne 3D LIDARs.

name: velodyne_pointcloud
description: Point cloud conversions for Velodyne 3D LIDARs.

This contains CvBridge, which converts between ROS2

name: image_geometry
description:
`image_geometry` contains C++ and Python libraries for interpreting images
    geometrically. It interfaces the calibration parameters in sensor_msgs/CameraInfo
    messages with OpenCV functions such as image rectification, much as cv_bridge
    interfaces ROS sensor_msgs/Image with OpenCV data types.

Xacro (XML Macros)
    Xacro is an XML macro language. With xacro, you can construct shorter and more readable XML files by using macros that expand to larger XML expressions.

name: librealsense2
description: Library for capturing data from the Intel(R) RealSense(TM) SR300 and D400 cameras. This effort was initiated to better support researchers, creative coders, and app developers in domains such as robotics, virtual reality, and the internet of things. Several often-requested features of RealSense(TM); devices are implemented in this project, including multi-camera capture.
authors: Sergey Dorodnicov Mark Horn Reagan Lopez Itay Carpis

name: libcurl_vendor
description: Wrapper around libcurl, it provides a fixed CMake module and an ExternalProject build of it.

name: resource_retriever
description:
This package retrieves data from url-format files such as http://,
   ftp://, package:// file://, etc., and loads the data into memory.
   The package:// url for ros packages is translated into a local
   file:// url.  The resourse retriever was initially designed to load
   mesh files into memory, but it can be used for any type of
   data. The resource retriever is based on the the libcurl library.

name: rqt_action
description:
rqt_action provides a feature to introspect all available ROS
  action types. By utilizing rqt_msg, the output format is
  unified with it and rqt_srv. Note that the actions shown on this plugin
  is the ones that are stored on your machine, not on the ROS core your rqt
  instance connects to.
maintainers: Mikael Arguedas Aaron Blasdel Geoffrey Biggs

name: rqt_bag

name: ament_cmake_core
description:
The core of the ament buildsystem in CMake.

    Several subcomponents provide specific funtionalities:
    * environment: provide prefix-level setup files
    * environment_hooks: provide package-level setup files and environment hooks
    * index: store information in an index and retrieve them without crawling
    * package_templates: templates from the ament_package Python package
    * symlink_install: use symlinks for CMake install commands

name: nav2_costmap_2d
description:
This package provides an implementation of a 2D costmap that takes in sensor
    data from the world, builds a 2D or 3D occupancy grid of the data (depending
    on whether a voxel based implementation is used), and inflates costs in a
    2D costmap based on the occupancy grid and a user specified inflation radius.
    This package also provides support for map_server based initialization of a
    costmap, rolling window based costmaps, and parameter based subscription to
    and configuration of sensor topics.
maintainers: Michael Ferguson Aaron Hoy Steve Macenski

name: nav2_voxel_grid
description: voxel_grid provides an implementation of an efficient 3D voxel grid. The occupancy grid can support 3 different representations for the state of a cell: marked, free, or unknown. Due to the underlying implementation relying on bitwise and and or integer operations, the voxel grid only supports 16 different levels per voxel column. However, this limitation yields raytracing and cell marking performance in the grid comparable to standard 2D structures making it quite fast compared to most 3D structures.

The actionlib stack provides a standardized interface for
    interfacing with preemptable tasks. Examples of this include moving
    the base to a target location, performing a laser scan and returning
    the resulting point cloud, detecting the handle of a door, etc.
authors: Eitan Marder-Eppstein Vijay Pradeep Mikael Arguedas

name: app_manager
description: app_manager
authors: Jeremy Leibs Ken Conley Yuki Furuta

name: arbotix
description: ArbotiX Drivers

name: arbotix_controllers
description: Extends the arbotix_python package with a number of more sophisticated ROS wrappers for common devices.

name: arbotix_firmware
description: Firmware source code for ArbotiX ROS bindings.

name: arbotix_msgs
description: Messages and Services definitions for the ArbotiX.

name: arbotix_python
description: Bindings and low-level controllers for ArbotiX-powered robots.

name: arbotix_sensors
description: Extends the arbotix_node package with a number of more sophisticated ROS wrappers for common devices.

name: diagnostic_aggregator
description: diagnostic_aggregator

name: diagnostic_analysis
description:
The diagnostic_analysis package can convert a log of diagnostics data
    into a series of CSV files. Robot logs are recorded with rosbag, and
    can be processed offline using the scripts in this package.
authors: Kevin Watts Brice Rebsamen Eric Berger Kevin Watts

name: diagnostic_common_diagnostics
description: diagnostic_common_diagnostics

name: diagnostic_updater
description: diagnostic_updater contains tools for easily updating diagnostics. it is commonly used in device drivers to keep track of the status of output topics, device status, etc.
authors: Kevin Watts Brice Rebsamen Jeremy Leibs Blaise Gassend

name: diagnostics
description: diagnostics
authors: Kevin Watts Brice Rebsamen Kevin Watts

name: rosdiagnostic
description: Command to print aggregated diagnostic contents to the command line

name: self_test
description: self_test
authors: Kevin Watts Brice Rebsamen Jeremy Leibs and Blaise Gassend

name: test_diagnostic_aggregator
description: Basic diagnostic_aggregator tests are  in the
authors: Kevin Watts Brice Rebsamen Kevin Watts

name: driver_base
description:
A framework for writing drivers that helps with runtime reconfiguration, diagnostics and self-test.

    This package is deprecated.

name: driver_common
description:
The driver_common stack contains classes and tools that are useful
    throughout the driver stacks. It currently contains:

    driver_base: A base class for sensors to provide a consistent state machine
    (retries, error handling, etc.) and interface

    timestamp_tools: Classes to help timestamp hardware events

name: timestamp_tools
description:
This package is currently for internal use only. Its API may change
    without warning in the future.  This package is deprecated.

name: dynamic_reconfigure
description:
This unary stack contains the dynamic_reconfigure package which provides a means to change
    node parameters at any time without having to restart the node.

name: dynpick_driver
description: Driver package for Wacohtech dynpick force sensor. This contains

name: ecl_config
description:
These tools inspect and describe your system with macros, types 
     and functions.

name: ecl_console
description: Color codes for ansii consoles.

name: ecl_converters_lite
description:
These are a very simple version of some of the functions in ecl_converters 
     suitable for firmware development. That is, there is no use of new, 
     templates or exceptions.

name: ecl_errors
description:
This library provides lean and mean error mechanisms. 
    It includes c style error functions as well as a few 
    useful macros. For higher level mechanisms, 
    refer to ecl_exceptions.

name: ecl_io
description:
Most implementations (windows, posix, ...) have slightly different api for 
     low level input-output functions. These are gathered here and re-represented 
     with a cross platform set of functions.

name: ecl_lite
description: Libraries and utilities for embedded and low-level linux development.

name: ecl_sigslots_lite
description:
This avoids use of dynamic storage (malloc/new) and thread safety (mutexes) to
     provide a very simple sigslots implementation that can be used for *very*
     embedded development.

name: ecl_time_lite
description:
Provides a portable set of time functions that are especially useful for 
     porting other code or being wrapped by higher level c++ classes.

name: ecl_build
description:
Collection of cmake/make build tools primarily for ecl development itself, but also
     contains a few cmake modules useful outside of the ecl.

name: ecl_license
description:
Maintains the ecl licenses and also provides an install
     target for deploying licenses with the ecl libraries.

name: ecl_tools
description: Tools and utilities for ecl development.

name: ecto
description: Ecto is a hybrid C++/Python development framework for constructing and maintaining pipelines.

name: eigen_stl_containers
description:
This package provides a set of typedef's that allow
  using Eigen datatypes in STL containers

name: camera_calibration_parsers
description: camera_calibration_parsers contains routines for reading and writing camera calibration parameters.

name: camera_info_manager
description:
This package provides a C++ interface for camera calibration
     information.  It provides CameraInfo, and handles SetCameraInfo
     service requests, saving and restoring the camera calibration
     data.

name: image_common
description: Common code for working with images in ROS.

name: image_transport
description:
image_transport should always be used to subscribe to and publish images. It provides transparent
     support for transporting images in low-bandwidth compressed formats. Examples (provided by separate
     plugin packages) include JPEG/PNG compression and Theora streaming video.

name: polled_camera
description:
polled_camera contains a service and C++ helper classes for implementing a polled
     camera driver node and requesting images from it. The package is currently for
     internal use as the API is still under development.

name: camera_calibration
description:
camera_calibration allows easy calibration of monocular or stereo
     cameras using a checkerboard calibration target.

name: depth_image_proc
description:
Contains nodelets for processing depth images such as those
     produced by OpenNI camera. Functions include creating disparity
     images and point clouds, as well as registering (reprojecting)
     a depth image into another camera frame.

name: image_pipeline
description: image_pipeline fills the gap between getting raw images from a camera driver and higher-level vision processing.

name: image_proc
description: Single image rectification and color processing.
authors: Patrick Mihelich Kurt Konolige Jeremy Leibs

name: image_publisher
image_rotate
image_view
description:
A simple viewer for ROS image topics. Includes a specialized viewer
  for stereo + disparity images.

name: stereo_image_proc
description: Stereo and single image rectification and disparity processing.
authors: Patrick Mihelich Kurt Konolige Jeremy Leibs

name: compressed_depth_image_transport
description:
Compressed_depth_image_transport provides a plugin to image_transport for transparently sending
    depth images (raw, floating-point) using PNG compression.

name: compressed_image_transport
description:
Compressed_image_transport provides a plugin to image_transport for transparently sending images
    encoded as JPEG or PNG.

name: image_transport_plugins
description:
A set of plugins for publishing and subscribing to sensor_msgs/Image topics
    in representations other than raw pixel data. For example, for viewing a
    stream of images off-robot, a video codec will give much lower bandwidth
    and latency. For low frame rate tranport of high-definition images, you
    might prefer sending them as JPEG or PNG-compressed form.

name: theora_image_transport
description:
Theora_image_transport provides a plugin to image_transport for
    transparently sending an image stream encoded with the Theora codec.

name: imu_pipeline
description: imu_pipeline

name: imu_processors
description: Processors for sensor_msgs::Imu data

name: imu_transformer
description: Node/nodelet combination to transform sensor_msgs::Imu data from one frame into another.

name: imu_complementary_filter
description: Filter which fuses angular velocities, accelerations, and (optionally) magnetic readings from a generic IMU device into a quaternion to represent the orientation of the device wrt the global frame. Based on the algorithm by Roberto G. Valenti etal. described in the paper "Keeping a Good Attitude: A Quaternion-Based Orientation Filter for IMUs and MARGs" available at http://www.mdpi.com/1424-8220/15/8/19302 .

name: imu_filter_madgwick
description: Filter which fuses angular velocities, accelerations, and (optionally) magnetic readings from a generic IMU device into an orientation. Based on code by Sebastian Madgwick, http://www.x-io.co.uk/node/8#open_source_ahrs_and_imu_algorithms.

name: imu_tools
description: Various tools for IMU devices

name: rviz_imu_plugin
description: RVIZ plugin for IMU visualization

name: innok_heros_driver
description: Driver for the Innok Heros robot plattform

name: interactive_marker_twist_server
description: Interactive control for generic Twist-based robots using interactive markers

name: interactive_markers
description: 3D interactive marker communication library for RViz and similar tools.

name: laser_assembler
description: Provides nodes to assemble point clouds from either LaserScan or PointCloud messages

name: laser_filtering
description: ROS Libraries for filtering specific kinds of laser scans

name: map_laser
description: Filters a laser scan to remove points that are in the current static map

name: laser_filters
description:
Assorted filters designed to operate on 2D planar laser scanners,
    which use the sensor_msgs/LaserScan type.

name: laser_geometry
description:
This package contains a class for converting from a 2D laser scan as defined by
    sensor_msgs/LaserScan into a point cloud as defined by sensor_msgs/PointCloud
    or sensor_msgs/PointCloud2. In particular, it contains functionality to account
    for the skew resulting from moving robots or tilting laser scanners.

name: laser_pipeline
description:
Meta-package of libraries for processing laser data, including converting laser data
      into 3D representations.

name: laser_proc
description: laser_proc

name: access_point_control
description:
Defines an API for access point control based on 
    dynamic_reconfigure. Other packages must
    implement the API for various access-point models: 
    for example: hostapd_access_point for hostapd-based control or
    linksys_access_point for Linksys router web interface.

name: asmach
description:
SMACH, which stands for 'state machine', is a task-level
    architecture for rapidly creating complex robot behavior. At its
    core, SMACH is a ROS-independent Python library to build
    hierarchical state machines.  SMACH is a new library that takes
    advantage of very old concepts in order to quickly create robust
    robot behavior with maintainable and modular code.

name: asmach_tutorials
description: This package containes numerous examples of how to use SMACH. See the examples directory.

name: ddwrt_access_point
description:
A ROS node that controls a Linksys WRT610Nv2 access point with
    a dd-wrt firmware. Other access points models/dd-wrt versions
    may be compatible as long as the web interface is identical.

name: hostapd_access_point
description:
A ROS node that controls a hostapd-based access
    point. It is mainly intended for use with a wireless 
    network adapter running in master mode. It implements 
    the dynamic_reconfigure interface defined
    in the [[access_point_control]] package.

name: ieee80211_channels
description:
This package provides mapping from frequencies to
    IEEE802.11 channels and vice-versa.

name: linksys_access_point
description:
A ROS node that controls a Linksys access point with
    a Linksys WRT610n-compatible web interface.

name: linux_networking
description: The linux_networking package

name: multi_interface_roam
description: sdsdddsdsds

name: network_control_tests
description:
Test suite for the packages that are part of the "WiFi Test Setup" project:
    network_monitor_udp, network_traffic_control, hostapd_access_point, linksys_access_point,
    ddwrt_access_point.

name: network_detector
description:
A ROS node that watches a given network interface and publishes
     whether it is both UP and RUNNING (indicating that a cable is
     plugged into it and communication is happening, for instance) or
     not.

name: network_monitor_udp
description:
Facilities to monitor a network connection by sending UDP packets from
    a client to a server, which bounces them back to the client. The client
    collects statistics on latency and loss. The server is a C standalone utility
    or a ROS node. The client can be a ROS node, a standalone utility or a python class.

name: network_traffic_control
description: A ROS node that allows control of network emulation parameters such as bandwidth, loss and latency for a Linux network interface.

name: log4cpp
description:
Log4cpp maintained by Orocos developers
    This version of log4cpp deviates from the official release
    by adding custom category factories. Orocos requires this for
    setting up real-time logging.
authors: Stephen Roderick, Bastiaan Bakker, Cedric Le Goater, Steve Ostlind, Marcel Harkema, Walter Stroebel, Glenn Scott and Tony Cheung

name: mavlink
description:
MAVLink message marshaling library.
  This package provides C-headers and C++11 library
  for both 1.0 and 2.0 versions of protocol.

  For pymavlink use separate install via rosdep (python-pymavlink).

name: libmavconn
description:
MAVLink communication library.
    This library provide unified connection handling classes
    and URL to connection object mapper.

    This library can be used in standalone programs.

tags: uav mav mavlink plugin apm px4 mavros
name: mavros
description:
MAVROS -- MAVLink extendable communication node for ROS
    with proxy for Ground Control Station.

name: mavros_extras
description: Extra nodes and plugins for

name: mavros_msgs
description: mavros_msgs defines messages for

name: test_mavros
description: Tests for MAVROS package

name: media_export
description: Placeholder package enabling generic export of media paths.

name: message_generation
description: Package modeling the build-time dependencies for generating language bindings of messages.

name: message_runtime
description: Package modeling the run-time dependencies for language bindings of messages.

name: desktop
description: A metapackage to aggregate several packages.

name: desktop_full
description: A metapackage to aggregate several packages.

name: perception
description: A metapackage to aggregate several packages.

name: robot
description: A metapackage which extends ros_base and includes ROS libaries for any robot hardware. It may not contain any GUI dependencies.

name: ros_base
description: A metapackage which extends ros_core and includes other basic non-robot tools like actionlib, dynamic reconfigure, nodelets, and pluginlib.

name: ros_core
description: A metapackage to aggregate the packages required to use publish / subscribe, services, launch files, and other core ROS concepts.

name: simulators
description: A metapackage to aggregate several packages.

name: viz
description: A metapackage to aggregate several packages.

name: microstrain_3dmgx2_imu
description:
A driver for IMUs compatible the microstrain 3DM-GX2 and 3DM-GX3 protocol. Includes 
    a heavily modified standalone driver pulled from the player distribution, 
    and a ROS node.

name: moveit_msgs
description: Messages, services and actions used by MoveIt
maintainers: Dave Coleman Michael Ferguson Michael Goerner Robert Haschke Isaac I. Y. Saito

name: moveit_python
description: A pure-python interaface to the MoveIt! ROS API.

name: moveit_resources
